title: Gateway timeout handling and retry configuration
author: contributor-eve

split_issues.title: Fix gateway timeout handling for large payloads
Add configurable retry policy for gateway proxy

assessments.reasoning: The timeout issue is a reliability defect. Large payloads exceed the default timeout, causing silent failures.

split_recommendation.title: Fix gateway timeout handling for large payloads
split_recommendation.description: The proxy uses a hardcoded 30s timeout that is insufficient for large payloads. Add size-aware timeout scaling.

assessments.reasoning: The issue conflates two distinct concerns: timeout handling (bug) and retry configuration (feature). These should be separate issues.

split_recommendation.title: Fix gateway timeout handling for large payloads
split_recommendation.description: Address the timeout defect with size-aware scaling.

split_recommendation.title: Add configurable retry policy for gateway proxy
split_recommendation.description: New feature: expose retry count, backoff, and jitter as configuration options.

assessments.reasoning: Both concerns are valid. The timeout is a defect; the retry config is a feature request. Splitting improves traceability.

split_recommendation.title: Fix gateway timeout handling for large payloads
split_recommendation.description: Bug fix for the hardcoded timeout.

split_recommendation.title: Add configurable retry policy for gateway proxy

assessments.reasoning: Both concerns affect operational reliability. The timeout fix is urgent; the retry config is important but can follow.
Splitting allows independent test strategies. Timeout fix needs regression tests; retry config needs integration tests.

child_issues_created.title: Fix gateway timeout handling for large payloads
